template_notes: This sample matches the app backup import format used by File > Import. Other platforms can generate the same keys and replace the sample values.

questions.question: What does Bernoulli's principle relate in steady incompressible flow?
questions.explanation: Bernoulli's principle links pressure, velocity, and elevation along a streamline.

questions.question: Which force acts perpendicular to the relative wind?
questions.explanation: Lift acts perpendicular to the relative wind.

questions.question: Which drag component becomes dominant at high angles of attack before stall?
questions.explanation: Induced drag increases significantly as lift coefficient rises.

questions.question: Why does increasing aircraft weight generally increase stall speed?
choices: Because heavier aircraft always reduce wing area in flight
Because more lift coefficient is required to balance weight
Because drag becomes zero near stall
Because thrust directly lowers the lift requirement
questions.answer_text: Because more lift coefficient is required to balance weight
questions.explanation: A heavier aircraft must produce more lift, which raises the required stall speed for the same configuration.

questions.question: During climb planning, which combination is usually important to monitor together?
choices: Rate of climb and excess power
Cabin lighting and flap handle texture
Marker beacon volume and tire pressure
questions.answer_text: Rate of climb and excess power
questions.explanation: Climb performance depends strongly on available excess power.
